author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXVII. 45-48.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the earth until the ninth hour.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, and said, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani? that is to say, my God my God, why hast thou forsaken me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Some of them that stood there, when they heard that said, this man calleth for Elias.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And straight way one of them ran, and took a sponge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave Him to drink.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is the sign which before He had promised to give them when they asked it, saying, “An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign, and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas;”meaning His cross, and His death, His burial, and His resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, declaring in another way the virtue of the cross, He said, “When ye have lifted up the Son of Man, then shall ye know that I am He.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what He saith is to this purport: “When ye have crucified me, and think ye have overcome me, then, above all, shall ye know my might.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after the crucifixion, the city was destroyed, and the Jewish state came to an end, they fell away from their polity and their freedom, the gospel flourished, the word was spread abroad to the ends of the world; both sea and land, both the inhabited earth and the desert perpetually proclaim itspower.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things then He meaneth, and those which took place at the very time of the crucifixion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed it was much more marvellous that these things should be done, when He was nailed to the cross, than when He was walking on earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not in this respect only was the wonder, but because from heaven also was that done which they had sought, and it was over all the world, which had never before happened, but in Egypt only, when the passover was to be fulfilled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed those events were a type of these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And observe when it took place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At midday, that all that dwell on the earth may know it, when it was day all over the world; which was enough to convert them, not by the greatness of the miracle only, but also by its taking place in due season.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after all their insulting, and their lawless derision, this is done, when they had let go their anger, when they had ceased mocking, when they were satiated with their jeerings, and had spoken all that they were minded; then He shows the darkness, in order that at least so (having vented their anger) they may profit by the miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this was more marvellous than to come down from the cross, that being on the cross He should work these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whether they thought He Himself had done it, they ought to have believed and to have feared; or whether not He, but the Father, yet thereby ought they to have been moved to compunction, for that darkness was a token of His anger at their crime.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that it was not an eclipse, but both wrath and indignation, is not hence alone manifest, but also by the time, for it continued three hours, but an eclipse takes place in one moment of time, and they know it, who have seen this; and indeed it hath taken place even in our generation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how, you may say, did not all marvel, and account Him to be God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because the race of man was then held in a state of great carelessness and vice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this miracle was but one, and when it had taken place, immediately passed away; and no one was concerned to inquire into the cause of it, and great was the prejudice and the habit of ungodliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they knew not what was the cause of that which took place, and they thought perhaps this happened so, in the way of an eclipse or some natural effect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why dost thou marvel about them that are without, that knew nothing, neither inquired by reason of great indifference, when even those that were in Judæa itself, after so many miracles, yet continued using Him despitefully, although He plainly showed them that He Himself wrought this thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for this reason, even after this He speaks, that they might learn that He was still alive, and that He Himself did this, and that they might become by this also more gentle, and He saith, “Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?”that unto His last breath they might see that He honors His Father, and is no adversary of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He uttered a certain cry from the prophet,even to His last hour bearing witness to the Old Testament, and not simply a cry from the prophet, but also in Hebrew, so as to be plain and intelligible to them, and by all things He shows how He is of one mind with Him that begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark herein also their wantonness, and intemperance, and folly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They thought (it is said) that it was Elias whom He called, and straightway they gave Him vinegar to drink.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But another came unto Him, and “pierced His side with a spear.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What could be more lawless, what more brutal, than these men; who carried their madness to so great a length, offering insult at last even to a dead body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou, I pray thee, how He made use of their wickednesses for our salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after the blow the fountains of our salvation gushed forth from thence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And Jesus, when He had cried with a loud voice, yielded up the Ghost.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is what He said, “I have power to lay down my life, and I have power to take it again,” and, “I lay it down of myself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So for this cause He cried with the voice, that it might be shown that the act is done by power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mark at any rate saith, that “Pilate marvelled if He were already dead:”and that the centurion for this cause above all believed, because He died with power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This cry rent the veil, and opened the tombs, and made the house desolate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He did this, not as offering insult to the temple (for how should He, who saith, “Make not my Father’s house a house of merchandise”),but declaring them to be unworthy even of His abiding there; like as also when He delivered it over to the Babylonians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not for this only were these things done, but what took place was a prophecy of the coming desolation, and of the change into the greater and higher state; and a sign of His might.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And together with these things He showed Himself also by what followed after these things, by the raising of the dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in the instance of Elisha; one on touching a dead body rose again, but now by a voice He raised them, His body continuing up there, on the cross.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, those things were a type of this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that this might be believed, therefore is that all done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they are not merely raised, but also rocks are rent, and the earth shaken, that they might learn, that He was able to strike themselves blind, and to rend them in pieces.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He that cleft rocks asunder, and darkened the world, much more could have done these things to them, had it been His will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He would not, but having discharged His wrath upon the elements, them it was His will to save by clemency.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they abated not their madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such is envy, such is jealousy, it is not easily stayed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At that time then they were impudent in setting themselves against the actual appearances; and afterwards even against the things themselves, when a seal being put upon Him, and soldiers watching Him, He rose again, and they heard these things from the very guards; they even gave money, in order both to corrupt others, and to steal away the history of the resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Marvel not therefore if at this time also they were perverse, being thus altogether prepared to set themselves impudently against all things; but observe this other point, how great signs He had wrought, some from Heaven, some on earth, some in the very temple, at once marking His indignation, and at the same time showing that what were unapproachable are now to be entered, and that Heaven shall be opened; and the work removed to the true Holy of Holies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they indeed said, “If He be the King of Israel, let Him come down now from the cross,”but He shows that He is King of all the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas those men said, “Thou that destroyest this temple, and buildest it in three days,” He shows that it shall be made forever desolate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again they said, “He saved others, Himself He cannot save,”but He while abiding on the cross proved this most abundantly on the bodies of His servants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if for Lazarus to rise on the fourth day was a great thing, how much more for all those who had long ago fallen asleep, at once to appear alive, which was a sign of the future resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “many bodies of the saints which slept, arose,” it is said, “and went into the holy city, and appeared to many.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in order that what was done might not be accounted to be an imagination, they appear, even to many, in the city.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the Centurion too then glorified God, saying, “Truly this was a righteous man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the multitudes that came together to that sight, returned beating their breasts.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So great was the power of the crucified, that after so many mockings, and scoffs, and jeers, both the centurion was moved to compunction, and the people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And some say that there is also a martyrdom of this centurion, who after these things grew to manhood in the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And many women were there beholding afar off, which had followed Him, ministering unto Him, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee’s sons.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things the women see done, these who were most inclined to feel for Him, who were most of all bewailing Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark how great their assiduity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They had followed Him ministering to Him, and were present even unto the time of the dangers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also they saw all; how He cried, how He gave up the ghost, how the rocks were rent, and all the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these first see Jesus; and the sex that was most condemned, this first enjoys the sight of the blessings, this most shows its courage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when the disciples had fled, these were present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But who were these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: His mother, for she is called mother of James,and the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But another evangelistsaith, that many also lamented over the things that were done, and smote their breasts, which above all shows the cruelty of the Jews, for that they gloried in things for which others were lamenting, and were neither moved by pity, nor checked by fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed the things that were done were of great wrath, and were not merely signs, but signs of anger all of them, the darkness, the cloven rocks, the veil rent in the midst, the shaking of the earth, and great was the excess of the indignation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But Joseph went, and begged the body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This was Joseph, who was concealing his discipleship of late; now however he had become very bold after the death of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither was he an obscure person, nor of the unnoticed; but one of the council, and highly distinguished; from which circumstance especially one may see his courage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he exposed himself to death, taking upon him enmity with all, by his affection to Jesus, both having dared to beg the body, and not having desisted until he obtained it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not by taking it only, nor by burying it in a costly manner, but also by laying it in his own new tomb, he showeth his love, and his courage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this was not so ordered without purpose, but so there should not be any bare suspicion, that one had risen instead of another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what purpose do these wait by it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As yet they knew nothing great, as was meet, and high about Him, wherefore also they had brought ointments, and were waiting at the tomb, so that if the madness of the Jews should relax, they might go and embrace the body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou women’s courage? seest thou their affection? seest thou their noble spirit in money? their noble spirit even unto death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us men imitate the women; let us not forsake Jesus in temptations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they for Him even dead spent so much and exposed their lives, but we (for again I say the same things) neither feed Him when hungry, nor clothe Him when naked, but seeing Him begging, we pass Him by.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet if ye saw Himself, every one would strip himself of all his goods.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But even now it is the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He Himself has said, I am he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then dost thou not strip thyself of all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed even now thou hearest Him say, Thou doest it unto me; and there is no difference whether thou givest to this man or to Him; thou hast nothing less than these women that then fed Him, but even much more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But be not perplexed!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not so much to have fed Him appearing in His own person, which would be enough to prevail with a heart of stone, as (because of His mere word) to wait upon the poor, the maimed, him that is bent down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in the former case, the look and the dignity of Him who appears divides with thee that which is done; but here the reward is entire for thy benevolence; and there is the proof of the greater reverence towards Him, when at His mere word waiting upon thy fellow-servant thou refreshest him in all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Refresh him, and believe Him, who receiveth it, and saith, Thou givest to me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For unless thou hadst given to Him, He would not have counted thee worthy of a kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou hadst not turned away from Him, He would not have sent thee to hell, if thou hadst overlooked a chance person; but because it is He Himself that is despised, therefore great is the blame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus also Paul persecuted Him, in persecuting them that are His; wherefore too He said, “Why persecutest thou me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus therefore let us feel, as bestowing on Christ Himself when we bestow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed His words are more sure than our sight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore thou seest a poor man, remember His words, by which He declared, that it is He Himself who is fed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though that which appears be not Christ, yet in this man’s form Christ Himself receiveth and beggeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But art thou ashamed to hear that Christ beggeth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Rather be ashamed when thou dost not give to Him begging of thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is shame, this is vengeance and punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since for Him to beg is of His goodness, wherefore we ought even to glory therein; but for thee not to give, is of thy inhumanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou believe not now, that in passing by a poor man that is a believer, thou passest by Him, thou wilt believe it then, when He will bring thee into the midst and say, “Inasmuch as ye did it not to these, ye did it not to me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But God forbid that we should so learn it, and grant rather that we may believe now, and bring forth fruit, and hear that most blessed voice that bringeth us into the kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But perhaps some one will say, “Thou art every day discoursing to us of almsgiving and humanity.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither will I cease to speak of this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if ye had attained to it, in the first place, not even so ought I to desist, for fear of making you the more remiss; yet had ye attained, I might have relaxed a little; but if ye have not arrived even at the half; say not these things to me, but to yourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed thou doest the same in blaming me, as if a little child, hearing often of the letter alpha, and not learning it, were to blame its teacher, because he is continually and for ever reminding him about it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who from these discourses has become more forward in the giving of alms?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who has cast down his money?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who has given the half of his substance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who the third part?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then should it be other than absurd, when ye do not learn, to require us to desist from teaching?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ye ought to do the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though we were minded to desist, ye ought to stop us and to say, we have not yet learnt these things, and how is it ye have desisted from reminding us of them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If it befell any one to suffer from his eye, and I happened to be a physician, and then having covered it up and anointed it, and having applied other treatment, I had not benefited it much, and so had desisted; would he not have come to the doors of my surgery and cried out against me, accusing me of great remissness, for that I had of myself withdrawn, while the disease remained; and if, on being blamed, I had said in reply to these things, that I had covered it up, and anointed it; would he have endured it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means, but would immediately have said; “And what is the advantage, if I still suffer pain.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Reason thus also with respect to thy soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what if after having often fomented a hand that was lifeless and shrunk, I had not succeeded in mollifying it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Should I not have heard the same thing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And even now a hand that is shrunk and withered we bathe, and for this reason, until we can stretch it out perfectly, we will not desist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Would that you too were to discourse of nothing else, at home and at market, at table and at night, and as a dream.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if we were always careful about these things by day, even in our dreams we should be engaged in them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sayest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Am I forever speaking of almsgiving?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I would wish myself that there were not great need for me to address this advice to you, but that I were to speak of the battle against the Jews, and heathens, and heretics; but when ye are not yet sound, how can any one arm you for the fight?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How should he lead you to the array, yet having wounds and gashes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since if indeed I saw you thoroughly sound in health, I should lead you forth to that battle array, and ye would see by the grace of Christ ten thousands lying dead, and their heads cast one upon another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In other books at any rate, many discourses have been spoken by us touching these things, but not even so are we able thoroughly to triumph in the victory, because of the remissness of the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when we conquer them ten thousand times over in doctrines, they reproach us with the lives of the multitude of those who join our congregations, their wounds, their diseases in their soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then shall we with confidence show you in the battle array, when ye rather do us mischief, being straightway wounded by our enemies, and made a mock of?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one man’s hand is diseased, and shrunk so as not to be able to give away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then should such a one hold a shield, and thrust it before him, and avoid being wounded by the jeers of cruelty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With others the feet halt, as many as go up to the theatres, and to the resorts of the harlot women.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How shall these then be able to stand in the battle, and not to be wounded with the accusation of wantonness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Another suffers and is maimed in his eyes, not looking straight, but being full of lasciviousness, and assailing women’s chastity, and overthrowing marriages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then should this man be able to look in the face of the enemy, and brandish a spear, and throw his dart, being goaded on all sides with jeers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: We may see also many suffering with the belly not less than the dropsical, when they are held in subjection by gluttony and drunkenness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then shall I be able to lead forth these drunken men to war?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With others the mouth is rotten; such are the passionate, and revilers, and blasphemers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then shall this man ever shout in battle, and achieve anything great and noble, he too being drunk with another drunkenness, and affording much laughter to the enemy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore each day I go about this camp, dressing your wounds, healing your sores.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if ye ever rouse yourselves up, and become fit even to wound others, I will both teach you this art of war, and instruct you how to handle these weapons, or rather your works themselves will be weapons to you, and all men will immediately submit, if ye would become merciful, if forbearing, if mild and patient, if ye would show forth all other virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if any gainsay, then we will also add the proof of what we can show on our part,bringing you forward, since now we rather are hindered (at least as to your part) in this race.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: We say that Christ hath done great things, having made angels of men; then, when we are called upon to give account, and required to furnish a proof out of this flock, our mouths are stopped.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I am afraid, lest in the place of angels, I bring forth swine as from a style, and horses mad with lust.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I know ye are pained, but not against you all are these things spoken, but against the guilty, or rather not even against them if they awake, but for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since now indeed all is lost and ruined, and the church is become nothing better than a stable of oxen, and a fold for asses and camels, and I go round seeking for a sheep, and am not able to see it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So much are all kicking, like horses, and any wild asses, and they fill the place here with much dung, for like this is their discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if indeed one could see the things spoken at each assemblage,by men, by women, thou wouldest see their words more unclean than that dung.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I entreat you to change this evil custom, that the church may smell of ointment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now, while we lay up in it perfumes for the senses, the uncleanness of the mind we use no great diligence to purge out, and drive away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the advantage?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we do not so much disgrace the church by bringing dung into it, as we disgrace it by speaking such things one to another, about gains, about merchandise, about petty tradings, about things that are nothing to us, when there ought to be choirs of angels here, and we ought to make the church a heaven, and to know nothing else but earnest prayers, and silence with listening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This then let us do at any rate, from the present time, that we may both purify our lives, and attain unto the promised blessings, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory world without end.
